sections.heading: Overview: Nevada OSHA and the State Plan

content.text: Nevada operates its own OSHA program — Nevada OSHA — under a federally approved State Plan. Nevada OSHA is administered by the Division of Industrial Relations (DIR), part of the Nevada Department of Business and Industry. Like other State Plan states, Nevada OSHA must be 'at least as effective' as federal OSHA, and in some areas Nevada has adopted additional or modified standards.
Contoso Retail's Nevada facilities (Las Vegas and Reno) must comply with Nevada OSHA standards for all workplace safety and health matters.

rows: Administers Nevada OSHA; sets safety standards
Conducts inspections, investigates complaints, issues citations
Free, confidential safety consultation for employers — no citations issued

sections.heading: Key Nevada OSHA Standards for Warehouse Operations

rows: Written accident prevention program required for all Nevada employers
No specific standard but Nevada OSHA actively enforces heat safety; see NV Heat Illness Prevention doc
GHS SDS and labeling requirements; see NV Hazard Communication doc
Written EAP required; reviewed with employees annually
Employer must conduct written PPE hazard assessment

content.text: Nevada requires every employer to develop and implement an Accident Prevention Program (APP) under NAC 618.538. The APP is Nevada's equivalent to California's IIPP and must include:

items: A general safety policy statement signed by the Warehouse Manager.
Identification of the person(s) responsible for safety.
Procedures for regular safety inspections of the workplace.

content.text: The Warehouse Manager is the designated APP Administrator for each Nevada facility. The APP is reviewed annually and following any serious incident. Nevada OSHA may request the APP during any inspection.
Nevada OSHA reporting requirements largely align with federal OSHA timelines:

rows: Available to Nevada OSHA on request

items: Right to request a Nevada OSHA inspection without employer knowledge.
Right to participate in an inspection by accompanying the inspector (walk-around rights).
Right to access injury/illness records and exposure monitoring results.
Right to file a complaint or report a safety violation without retaliation.
Nevada Revised Statutes (NRS) 618.445 prohibits employer retaliation against employees who exercise their Nevada OSHA rights.
